name: Healthcare, food & staples
description: Health-care prices & employment plus agricultural/food prices for healthcare, pharma, and consumer staples.

series.note: Broadest measure of medical-care inflation borne by consumers, capturing services plus commodities and signaling pricing power across the healthcare value chain.
Isolates inflation in physician, hospital, and insurance services, the demand/pricing signal most relevant to provider and managed-care revenue.
National-accounts measure of total consumer health-care spending, the best high-level proxy for sector demand and national health expenditure.
Labor-demand gauge for the broad health-and-education super-sector, reflecting staffing-driven cost and capacity trends.
Direct headcount in health-care delivery, signaling sector expansion, labor-cost inflation, and wage pressure for providers.
Hospital-specific employment, a leading indicator of acute-care capacity, labor cost, and admissions volume trends.
Wholesale (producer) drug-price inflation, capturing manufacturer pricing power upstream of retail and isolating pharma margin dynamics.
Industry-level producer price index for drug manufacturing, a precise input/output price signal for pharmaceutical makers.
Wholesale price index for processed food and animal feed, a direct input-cost gauge for packaged-food and protein companies.
Producer price index for grains, a primary raw-material cost driver for food, feed, ethanol, and beverage producers.
Restaurant-menu price inflation, a direct pricing-power and demand signal for foodservice and quick-service chains.
Closely watched staple-protein retail price, a sensitive read on supply shocks (e.g., avian flu) feeding grocer and food-cost narratives.
Global food-commodity benchmark, a macro input-cost and demand signal for multinational food and ag companies.
World wheat price, a core grain-input cost and a barometer of food-security and ag-trade conditions.
World corn price, a key feed/ethanol/sweetener input cost driving protein, ethanol, and beverage margins.
Wholesale beverage-input price index, a cost-pressure gauge for soft-drink, alcohol, and bottling companies.
Direct demand signal for grocery channel volume/value, the top-line driver for food retailers and a proxy for staples consumption.
Demand gauge for pharmacy and personal-care retail, informing front-of-store and prescription volume trends.
